steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "mustang" (a wild horse) comes from the Spanish word mesteñas, and "rodeo" (a ______ comes from rodear. An opening parenthesis before a ______ has no closing parenthesis yet. The blank must close that parenthetical, just as (a wild horse) is closed earlier in the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'roundup,': ends with a comma inside an open parenthesis — the parenthetical is never closed. ✗
  • Choice B — 'roundup)': the closing parenthesis matches the opening one, mirroring (a wild horse) exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'roundup;': a semicolon cannot close a parenthetical; the parenthesis remains open. ✗
  • Choice D — 'roundup—': an em-dash cannot close a parenthetical; it also mixes punctuation styles. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
